title: Volume of rectangular prisms
description: Find volume of a rectangular prism with labeled whole number side lengths.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of the rectangular prism?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^3$
hints: We want to know how many one-centimeter cubes can we fit in the prism.

### Step 1: Find the area of the base

First, let's figure out how many fit at the bottom (base) of the shape.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can fit $6\times 5=30$ unit cubes at the base.

**Note:** The base of this prism is a rectangle, which is why we call it a rectangular prism. The area of the base is $30 \text{ cm}^2$.

### Step 2: Multiply by the height of the prism

One layer of cubes has $30$ cubes. But, how many layers fit in the prism?



[[☃ image 1]]

We can fit $7$ layers of cubes in the prism because its height is $7\text{ cm}$.

Multiplying, we get

$30\times 7=210$ cubes

which means the volume of the prism is $210\text{ cm}^3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The answer:

$210 \text{ cm}^3$

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The base is 6 centimeters long and 5 centimeters wide. The height is 7 centimeters high.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of the rectangular prism?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^3$
hints: We want to know how many one-centimeter cubes can we fit in the prism.

### Step 1: Find the area of the base

First, let's figure out how many fit at the bottom (base) of the shape.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can fit $3\times 4=12$ unit cubes at the base.

**Note:** The base of this prism is a rectangle, which is why we call it a rectangular prism. The area of the base is $12 \text{ cm}^2$.

### Step 2: Multiply by the height of the prism

One layer of cubes has $12$ cubes. But, how many layers fit in the prism?

[[☃ image 1]]

We can fit $6$ layers of cubes in the prism because its height is $6\text{ cm}$.

Multiplying, we get

$12\times 6=72$ cubes

which means the volume of the prism is $72\text{ cm}^3$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The answer:

$72 \text{ cm}^3$

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The base is 3 centimeters long and 4 centimeters wide. The height is 6 centimeters high.
